opts: Keep carrying the puck in at full speed until the trap finally breaks down and lets you by
Pull the puck back to your defensemen, use a D-to-D pass to overload one side, or try a stretch pass to a breaking forward
Dump the puck deep every single rush and grind them down with a heavy forecheck eventually
Skate around the outside of the trap along the boards and curl back into the middle with speed
Carry the puck straight up the middle every time since you have the extra man advantage anyway
Use the extra man advantage: hold the puck until one PK player commits, then exploit what they've given up
Dump the puck deep into the corner and send all five skaters in hard to chase and recover it
Skate back into your own zone and regroup over and over until the PK gets tired and breaks
Both defenders chase the puck carrier hard and try to strip the puck before they get a shot off
Take your side — one defender takes the carrier, the other takes the second attacker — and protect the middle
Both defenders collapse down into the crease and pack the net to block every shot attempt
Skate straight back to the goal line and let the goalie handle the 2-on-2 rush on their own
Stand still in your spot and call loudly for the puck so your teammate knows you are open
Skate to a new open spot — cut to the net or find a seam. Your defender will have to follow.
Skate over toward your covered teammate to give them a close support pass option quickly
Wait right where you are until the pressure clears so you don't crowd your teammate out
Skate all the way into the corner to help your teammate battle for the loose puck down low
Close on the point player to pressure the shot or take away the dangerous pass options
Stay in the middle of the ice to block the cross-seam pass and protect the slot area well
Back all the way up to your own crease to help the goalie see shots through heavy traffic
Force a hard pass to one of the covered wingers and hope they can win the battle down the wall
Reverse behind the net — the forechecker on the left has to follow, which buys time for the center to get back
Carry the puck straight out yourself and try to skate through the forechecker coming from the left side
Shoot the puck down the ice and take the icing whistle to get a fresh faceoff in your own zone
Yes — always pinch hard and join every attack so you can keep the puck deep in the zone
Only if the other winger or center is positioned to cover the blue line behind you — otherwise hold the line
Yes — pinch every single time your winger touches the puck, no matter who is covering behind you
No — never pinch from the blue line under any circumstances because it is always too risky
Everyone chase the puck carrier at the same time to surround them and force a quick turnover
Hold your positions and make the puck carrier make a decision — force them one way and take away the easiest options
All four players skate backwards to your own blue line to protect the net area immediately
One player steps up to challenge while the other three retreat all the way back to the crease
Skate down into the corner to help your partner battle for the puck and win it back quickly
Hold the high slot — be between the corner player and the open attackers in the high-danger area
Go all the way to your blue line
Follow the nearest attacker wherever they go around the zone and stay glued to them tightly
Nothing at all — players should just react to where the puck goes after the official drops it
Their assignment — who they're covering and where they're going based on whether you win or lose the draw
Where the nearest door to the bench is in case their shift ends right after the puck drops
To skate as fast as they possibly can toward the puck the moment it hits the ice surface
Stay right where you are and trust that your defenseman will figure out the play on their own
Skate to the wall hard, using your speed to lose the forechecker for a split second — make the D's decision easy
Don't break to the wall until the forechecker has already left your area and given up the chase
Stand completely still in your spot and call loudly for the puck to get your D's attention
Backing up too fast and giving the attackers too much free space between the blue lines
Pinching up — closing aggressively on the puck carrier at the offensive zone instead of backing up
Staying in the middle of the ice and not moving to one side to take a specific attacker away
Talking to their partner during the rush instead of just skating back in total silence
Waiting with your stick flat on the ice, ready to catch and control the puck cleanly on contact
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so your teammate knows you are ready
Watching the puck travel the whole way to make sure that you don't miss it when it arrives
Keep skating toward the offensive zone and try to create your own chance before help arrives
Recognize the 2-on-1 developing and get the puck to your D immediately so they can deal with the threat
Dump the puck deep into the offensive zone right away and change lines for fresh legs to come
Stop where you are and wait for your teammates to catch up before making your next decision
Give up on seeing the puck and just hope the shot hits some part of your equipment cleanly
Move laterally to try to find a sightline, and be ready to react to tips and deflections
Skate hard into the screener to knock them out of the way before the shot gets released
Call for your teammates to clear the screener and wait for them to move the player away
Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to poke the puck away before the pass can be made
Take away the pass — stay in the middle, force the shooter, and let the goalie handle the shot
Chase the trailing player and leave
Back up all the way to your own crease and let the goalie handle the two attackers alone
What is the current score of the game and how much time is left on the clock right now?
Which outlet is actually open — not which one I want to use, but which one is actually free?
Where is the nearest bench door so I can get a fresh change of players out on the ice now?
What did the coach say about the breakout during the pre-game meeting in the locker room?
Rush hard at the puck carrier to try to strip the puck before they get into your zone
Take your side and force the carrier wide — your partner protects the middle attacker
Chase the trailing attacker down the far side and leave the puck carrier to your partner
Back up all the way to your crease
Right away as soon as they cross your blue line — drop into the butterfly immediately and wait
Wait until they've made their move — commit when you see what they're going to do, not before
Never go down at all — always stay up on your skates no matter what the shooter chooses to do
As soon as they cross the blue line so you are ready for the shot before it gets released
Try to kick the puck away with one foot while standing up on your skates the whole time
Drop into butterfly position — both knees down, pads flared out to cover the bottom
Fall sideways quickly
Stand straight up and hope that the shot hits you somewhere on your leg pads or chest
Shoot — you have the puck and a decent angle, so take the shot before anything changes
Pass to the slot player — the higher danger location is worth the slight coverage risk
Hold the puck and wait for a better chance
Pass all the way back to the point and let the defenseman set up a new play from there
Stay on your near post
Quickly push across to take away the back-door pass — your near post is less dangerous
Skate out hard toward the puck carrier to challenge them right at the top of the crease
Stay exactly where you are
Stay exactly where you are in the offensive zone and wait to see if the turnover really happens
Start transitioning early — turn toward your own zone before the turnover is complete. Get a head start.
Skate to the bench
Go to the corner
Take a big slap shot immediately on the faceoff draw to try to extend your lead early
Control the puck, cycle below the dots, use every second — only shoot on a high-quality chance
Dump the puck deep into the far corner and chase it hard with all your forwards
Pass the puck all the way back to your own zone to be safe and kill more clock
Skate down into the corner and try to re-win the puck battle from the opposing winger
Seal off the passing lane to the slot — get between the puck carrier and the dangerous area
Skate to your blue line and hope your teammate wins the battle in the corner
Watch the puck carrier closely and only react after the pass has already been made
Stay right where you are in case the shot misses wide and comes out to your side cleanly
Crashing the net hard on the weak side — be there for the rebound before the goalie can cover
Back up all the way to your own blue line to protect against a breakaway the other way
Call loudly for a pass instead of the shot so you can get a better chance from your side
Wait until both forecheckers have arrived before deciding which outlet pass to try to make
Look up immediately and read which outlet is open before the pressure arrives — decide early
Rim the puck around the boards no matter what the forecheckers are doing on that play
Skate out of the zone with the puck no matter what pressure is coming from the forecheckers
Ignore the attacker completely and trust that your partner will just follow them over
Call switch and take the attacker coming to your side so your partner can recover
Call for the referee to whistle a penalty on the crossing play
Back up all the way to the crease and let the attacker set up wherever they want to go
Shoot from where you are — any shot on net is a good shot from this angle for sure
Pass across to your teammate at the far post — the goalie can't get there in time
Skate to center ice and take the shot
Dump the puck behind the net and go fight for it in the corner with your teammate
Force the pass through the defender — your teammate is in the slot and you can see them
Find a better option — a covered pass through a defender is high-risk. Look for the winger or the point.
Hold the puck and skate around
Take the shot from where you
Stay flat on your goal line
Skate out toward the shooter to cut the angle — give them less net to shoot at
Skate all the way out past your blue line to challenge the shooter far from the net
Lie flat down on the ice right away and hope the shooter misses the net entirely
Shoot a hard wrist shot blocker side from way out before the goalie finishes setting up
Pull the puck wide, make the goalie commit to one side, then shoot or go to the other side
Try a quick straight backhand shot right down the middle before the goalie can get set
Slow way down and wait until you are right on top of the goalie before making a move
Stop them cold by skating straight at them and poking the puck off their stick quickly
Angle your body to steer them along the boards and away from the middle of the ice
Back up all the way to your crease and let the goalie handle it
Wait for them to shoot from a bad angle and then block the shot with your body fast
Skate out of the way so the goalie can see the puck clearly and make an easy save there
Stay planted and block the goalie's sightline — screen them, and be ready to tip the shot
Skate to the corner to get position early for the rebound off the boards
Call loudly for a pass instead of the shot so you can score from a better angle
Pass to your teammate just as planned — stick with it
Shoot — the goalie has moved and there's a lane on your side
Pass back to the other side again
Pull up and regroup back
Carry the puck in at full speed and try to blow right past the defender with pure speed
Chip the puck into the zone behind the defender and skate hard to get it first
Stop at the blue line and wait for your teammates
Pass it back to the D and regroup
Try to hold the puck and fight through both PK forwards to get a shot from the top
Move the puck quickly across to the half-wall before both pressure players arrive
Take a slap shot from distance through all the traffic and hope for a rebound chance
Pass the puck all the way back to your own zone and start the whole power play over
Stay with your original assignment and completely ignore the switch call from your partner
Take the attacker cutting to your side, let your partner recover their original man
Call the referee for a penalty
Back up all the way to the crease and try to cover both of the attackers at once
Top right — shoot high over their shoulder on the same side they are leaning hard toward
Low left — the opposite side of where they're leaning. They can't recover in time.
Right at their chest — power wins
Wait for the goalie to reset their position before you decide to release the shot
Pass out to a teammate on the perimeter
Shoot quickly to the left where the goalie is out of position
Stickhandle and wait for a much better angle
Turn and skate back out
Force a pass to a covered winger anyway
Walk out from behind the net alone and skate
Pass across to the open defenseman to reset the breakout
Shoot the puck hard around the boards and change
Hold the puck and wait for a better option to open up
One-time it or shoot immediately into the open slot
Pass the puck back to the other point
Skate into the zone yourself with it
